markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ confirmation of GTA VI’s November 19, 2026 release date has extended the timeline to roughly five months from now, turning the Polymarket into a venue for traders to wager on intervening cultural and industry milestones. With the marketing campaign expected to ramp up in late June—including the likely arrival of Trailer 3 and pre-order availability—attention has shifted to concurrent entertainment releases and global events that could resolve before launch. High-probability outcomes reflect near-term certainties in music and tech, while geopolitical resolutions carry more uncertainty tied to ongoing developments. Traders monitor chart trajectories, album announcements, and official statements for momentum shifts ahead of the game’s console debut.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 15, 2026 hung jury on the Jessica Mann rape charge, have solidified trader consensus around an 80% implied probability for no additional prison time. Prosecutors now face a late-June decision on pursuing a fourth trial after three attempts failed to deliver a verdict on the core allegation. Weinstein, now 74 and in declining health with cancer and mobility issues, remains incarcerated under his separate 16-year California sentence while appealing that conviction. Industry observers note that protracted #MeToo cases often stall at this stage, and his advanced age reduces the practical likelihood of extended consecutive terms even if convicted again. A June 24 status hearing could clarify whether further proceedings will proceed.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights remains tightly contested at 2-2, with the Hurricanes holding a modest edge in trader consensus due to home-ice advantage for Game 5 and momentum from their 5-3 road victory in Game 4. Carolina’s balanced attack, highlighted by Jordan Staal’s two goals and strong contributions from Nikolaj Ehlers plus rookie goaltender Brandon Bussi, helped erase deficits and even the series after Vegas took a 2-1 lead with a double-overtime win in Game 3. Both clubs advanced by overpowering their Eastern and Western Conference Final opponents, showcasing depth and resilience in a high-event series marked by multiple multi-goal comebacks. The current pricing reflects the slim margins typical of a best-of-seven tied at two games apiece heading into a home matchup.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Champion market after rallying from a 29-point deficit to win Game 4 of the Finals 107-106 at Madison Square Garden, seizing a 3-1 series lead. Their 53-29 regular-season mark, third-place Eastern Conference finish, and playoff resilience—bolstered by Jalen Brunson’s scoring and Karl-Anthony Towns’ interior presence—have driven sustained trader confidence. The San Antonio Spurs, at 19%, reached the Finals with a league-best 62-20 record and Victor Wembanyama’s Defensive Player of the Year campaign but now trail after squandering a large lead in Game 4. The young Western Conference champions retain upset potential in a potential Game 5 or 6 at home, though the Knicks’ current momentum and home-court edge in the series have shifted consensus sharply in New York’s favor.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Champion market after rallying from a 29-point deficit to win Game 4 of the Finals 107-106 at Madison Square Garden, seizing a 3-1 series lead. Their 53-29 regular-season mark, third-place Eastern Conference finish, and playoff resilience—bolstered by Jalen Brunson’s scoring and Karl-Anthony Towns’ interior presence—have driven sustained trader confidence. The San Antonio Spurs, at 19%, reached the Finals with a league-best 62-20 record and Victor Wembanyama’s Defensive Player of the Year campaign but now trail after squandering a large lead in Game 4. The young Western Conference champions retain upset potential in a potential Game 5 or 6 at home, though the Knicks’ current momentum and home-court edge in the series have shifted consensus sharply in New York’s favor.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges France as the narrowest favorite in this tightly bunched field, driven by La Roja's sustained momentum from their Euro 2024 title and a core featuring Lamine Yamal plus other young talents, while Les Bleus counter with elite depth headlined by Kylian Mbappe. England, Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina sit close behind, each bolstered by proven international pedigrees, strong qualifying records, and key performers like Jude Bellingham or Lionel Messi in what is likely his final World Cup. The expanded 48-team format and imminent group-stage kickoff amplify parity, with squad announcements, recent friendlies, and home-soil advantages for co-hosts adding layers of unpredictability that keep implied probabilities clustered among the leading contenders.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military as of mid-2026, driving the 92.3% implied probability against his removal before 2027. He has actively shaped the 15th Five-Year Plan for 2026-2030, conducted high-level diplomacy including a May summit with U.S. President Trump, and shows no signs of health constraints or internal challenges. Loyalist appointments and ongoing purges of officials reinforce centralized authority, while potential successors remain unpromoted to the Politburo Standing Committee ahead of the 21st Party Congress. Earlier 2025 rumors of decline or transition lacked substantiation and did not alter leadership dynamics. This consensus reflects trader assessments of structural continuity over imminent change.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 23% amid a fragmented field more than a year after the 2024 election, reflecting his high national profile as California governor, fundraising capacity, and early positioning in key states. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff follow in single digits, supported by progressive mobilization, prior national exposure, and Senate visibility respectively. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail further, buoyed by swing-state records and party infrastructure. Trader consensus highlights name recognition, primary-state polling trends, and potential 2026 midterm performance as key differentiators. Consolidation could occur through endorsements from party leaders, strong debate showings, or shifts in early-state voter coalitions before formal 2027-2028 primary contests begin.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 23% amid a fragmented field more than a year after the 2024 election, reflecting his high national profile as California governor, fundraising capacity, and early positioning in key states. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff follow in single digits, supported by progressive mobilization, prior national exposure, and Senate visibility respectively. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail further, buoyed by swing-state records and party infrastructure. Trader consensus highlights name recognition, primary-state polling trends, and potential 2026 midterm performance as key differentiators. Consolidation could occur through endorsements from party leaders, strong debate showings, or shifts in early-state voter coalitions before formal 2027-2028 primary contests begin.
